title: Pleuropterus multiflorus

intro: Pleuropterus multiflorus, also known as Korean angelica or multiflora pleuropterus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleuropterus multiflorus include essential oils, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tonics, which are commonly used in East Asian medicine to promote digestion and relieve menstrual discomfort.
